_README.purpose: Auto-generated mapping of raw value -> label for every active customlist_* in the account. Generated weekly by customscript_eg_mr_clv. Hand-edits will be overwritten on next run.
_README.format: top-level keys = customlist scriptid. Each entry has: title (CustomList.name), description (CustomList.description), scriptId, internalId, fromBundle, source ("auto"), lastUpdated (CustomList.lastmodifieddate — diff between runs to detect list changes), values: [{id, label}].

customlist_11664_payment_mode.title: Payment Mode
customlist_11664_payment_mode.description: Used to source the payment mode values i.e. In-transit and Simple.

customlist_12194_processing_result.title: Localization Processing Result
customlist_12194_processing_result.description:
This list contains the status of the payment template request.

Passed – This status indicates that the payment file template request is successful.

Failed – This status is displayed when the payment file template request has not been generated successfully due to errors encountered while processing.

customlist_2663_output_file_encoding.title: EP Output File Encoding
customlist_2663_output_file_encoding.description: Based on NetSuite's accepted encoding for a file

customlist_8299_license_cache_status.title: License Cache Status
customlist_8299_license_cache_status.description:
License Cache Status:
ACTIVE
STALE - when license cache record is replaced by a new record (by pulling/requesting new license data)
UNKNOWN- when the server is not responding (after n retries) and if license data has been tampered with

customlist_fam_asset_split_rules.title: FAM Asset Split Rules
customlist_fam_asset_split_rules.description:
Choose the rule to apply when you split an asset:
                • Equal Split: Enter a single split quantity to allocate the same number of assets for each record. For example, the original asset has a quantity of 50. If you enter 10, the asset will be split into 5 separate records each with a quantity of 10.
                • Custom Split: Enter multiple split quantities to allocate a different number of assets for each record. For example, the original asset has a quantity of 50. You can enter 10, 20, 20 to split the asset into 3 separate records each containing the specified quantity.

The values of the list will be used on various conditions across the workflow to ensure a Bill goes through the right approval flow.

values.label: Non-standard invoicing : Rent that is invoiced quarterly, half-yearly or yearly or seasonal variations in the business
Accruals : Corrections due to post-payment invoicing, previously omitted or forgotten invoices, or annual account dispositions
Previously used VAT code is incorrect : Correction due to reporting under an incorrect VAT code
Errors in accounting software : Correction of turnover due to previous errors in accounting software
Insurance settlement : VAT has been deducted in relation to an insurance settlement concerning the business
Turnover before registration : Turnover before registration in the Value Added Tax Register
Recalculation or returns : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Temporary import : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Re-import : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Customs declaration made under an incorrect organisation number : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Re-export or returns : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Temporary export : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Export of services : Explanation of differences between reports from Customs (customs declarations) and the tax return for VAT
Large purchases : Explanation of large deductions for input VAT
Credit notes : Invoice is used to change all or parts of a previous invoice
Purchases made before VAT registration : Purchases made before registration in the VAT register, retrospective VAT refunds or explanation of large deductions for input VAT
Building for own expense and risk : Goods and services withdrawn from the enterprise before the building is sold
Withdrawal of goods after cessation : Withdrawal of goods after cessation of the enterprise
Real property : Reversal of VAT on assets related to real property
Passenger vehicles : Reversal of VAT on assets related to passenger vehicles
Demonstration car : Calculation of output tax on car that is deducted and registered in the enterprise
Binding advance ruling : The Tax Administration’s binding advance ruling (BFU) will be used as the basis
Enterprise under liquidation : The enterprise will be deleted in the VAT register
The enterprise has changed legal structure : The enterprise is transferred to/from another organisation number
Drop in revenue due to extraordinary circumstances : Lower revenue than is usual for the enterprise
Shipwreck : Payment in the event of shipwreck, etc.
VAT-free sale of fishing vessel : VAT-free sale of fishing vessel
applying a discount that reduces the price of a product or service / zastosowanie rabatu obniżającego cenę produktu lub usługi
refund of all or part of the payment / zwrot całości lub części zapłaty
a mistake in the amounts listed on the invoice / pomyłka w kwotach wymienionych na fakturze

customlist_plp_jpk_v7_adj_legal_basis.title: PLP VAT adjustment legal basis
customlist_plp_jpk_v7_adj_legal_basis.description:
Selecting any of:
- art. 89a ust. 1
- art. 89a ust. 4
- art. 89b ust. 1
- art. 89b ust. 4
has an effect of just more detailed equivalent of previous VAT-ZD functionality. Please remember that VAT-ZD checkbox has no effect as of JPK_V7. On transactions to be reported in JPK_V7 structure, use this list instead.

values.label: art. 89a ust. 1 - downward adjustment of tax basis and tax due
art. 89a ust. 4 - upward adjustment of tax basis and tax due
art. 89b ust. 1 - downward adjustment of tax basis and input tax
art. 89b ust. 4 - upward adjustment of tax basis and input tax
art. 90a - downward or upward adjustment of input tax
art. 90b - downward or upward adjustment of input tax
art. 90c - downward or upward adjustment of input tax
art. 91 - downward or upward adjustment of input tax
11. Purchase/sale against payment (without qualified to natures started from 2,7,8)
12. Sale after acceptance, viewing or trying and transactions involving commission (without qualified to codes started from 2,7,8)
13. Transactions involving payment in kind (without qualified to codes started from 2,7,8)
14. Financial leasing (without qualified to codes started from 2,7,8)
19. Other transactions against payment (without qualified to codes started from 2,7,8)
22. Replacement for returned goods without payment
23. Replacement (e.g. under terms of guarantee) without payment for goods not returned
29. Other replacement or return of goods without payment
30. Transactions, which are connected with the change of ownership without financial or other compensation (eg. humanitarian shipments)
41. Transactions with a view to processing under contract - goods expected to return to the initial country of dispatch
42. Transactions with a view to processing under contract - goods not expected to return to the initial country of dispatch
51. Transactions after processing under contract - goods returning to the initial country of dispatch
52. Transactions after processing under contract - goods not returning to the initial country of dispatch
70. Transactions in connection with joint defence programmes or other intergovernmental production programmes (e.g. Airbus)
80. Delivery of building material and equipment in connection with construction or civil engineering as a part of a general contract
91. Other transactions - rent, loan, and operational leasing longer than 24 months
1. zwrot podatnikowi, który dokonywał dostawy towarów lub świadczył usługi poza terytorium kraju i nie dokonywał sprzedaży opodatkowanej
2. zwrot podatnikowi, który nie dokonywał w okresie rozliczeniowym czynności opodatkowanych na terytorium kraju oraz czynności, o których mowa w art. 86 ust. 8 pkt 1 ustawy
3. zwrot podatnikowi, który dokonywał nabycia (importu) towarów lub usług finansowanych ze środków bezzwrotnej pomocy zagranicznej
4. w innych przypadkach niż wymienione w pkt 1 – 3, jeżeli wniosek o zwrot składany jest zgodnie z przepisami o podatku od towarów i usług, wraz z deklaracją podatkową, a kwota wnioskowanego
